title: Just So
tagline: Rudyard Kipling's beloved stories brought to life on stage
synopsis: An adaptation of Rudyard Kipling's classic Just So Stories, exploring how various animals acquired their distinctive features — the kangaroo's legs, the cat's solitary nature, the rhino's itchy skin, and the elephant's trunk. With a large, flexible cast and episodic structure perfect for young performers, Just So is an ideal choice for elementary and middle school programs looking for a show that gives every student a meaningful role.
source_material: Just So Stories by Rudyard Kipling
cast_notes: 25 speaking roles plus flexible chorus; most roles are gender-neutral
licensing_includes: Complete script (printable PDF — unlimited copies for your cast)
Vocal lead sheets for every song
Backing tracks for rehearsal and performance
Video recording and YouTube rights included
No script rentals. No returns. You keep everything.
premiere_venue: Open Door Repertory Company, Oak Park, Illinois
production_history: Produced coast to coast across the United States

faq.a: No. Complete backing tracks are included for both rehearsal and performance. If you have a pianist, the piano/vocal score is included as well.

faq.q: Can I adjust the cast size?
faq.a: Yes. The 25 speaking roles are fixed, but the chorus is completely flexible — you can have as few as 5 or as many as 50 without changing the script.

faq.q: Is this show appropriate for elementary students?
faq.a: Absolutely. Just So is designed for 3rd grade and up. The episodic structure, age-appropriate humor, and gender-neutral roles make it ideal for elementary programs.
Yes. Just So requires minimal staging and works well in non-traditional spaces. The storytelling format adapts to any environment.

faq.q: How long is the rehearsal period?
faq.a: Most programs rehearse for 6-8 weeks. The episodic structure allows you to rehearse scenes independently, which is helpful when you can't get the full cast together at every rehearsal.

title: Becky Thatcher
tagline: Mark Twain's Hannibal, MO reimagined from Becky's perspective
synopsis: A stage adaptation of Mark Twain's classic, reimagined from Becky Thatcher's perspective. Features familiar adventures like the whitewash scene while centering the girls of Hannibal, Missouri. With more female roles than male and flexible gender-switching, Becky Thatcher is a fresh take on an American classic — Tom Sawyer with none of the controversy and all of the adventure.
source_material: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer by Mark Twain
cast_notes: 25 speaking roles plus flexible chorus. More female roles than male; gender can be switched for many parts.
licensing_notes: Unlimited performances within a 2-week calendar period
licensing_includes: Complete script (printable PDF — unlimited copies for your cast)
Vocal lead sheets for every song
Backing tracks for rehearsal and performance
Video recording and YouTube rights included
No script rentals. No returns. You keep everything.
notable_productions: The Actors Garden, Oak Park, Illinois
curriculum_connections: Language Arts — Mark Twain, literary adaptation
Social Studies — gender roles and perspectives

faq.q: How is this different from other Tom Sawyer musicals?
faq.a: Becky Thatcher tells the story from Becky's perspective, centering the female characters. It's a fresh take on familiar material with more roles for girls — and none of the controversial elements that make some Twain adaptations difficult for schools.

faq.q: Can boys play in this show?
faq.a: Absolutely. There are strong male roles including Tom Sawyer, Huck Finn, and others. The show leans female but gender can be switched for many parts.
No. Complete backing tracks are included. A piano/vocal score is also provided if you have a pianist.

faq.q: Is this appropriate for elementary students?
faq.a: Yes, for 4th grade and up. The themes of friendship, adventure, and standing up for yourself resonate with upper elementary and middle school students.

faq.q: Can I adjust the cast size?
faq.a: The 25 speaking roles are designed into the script, but the flexible chorus lets you include as many additional students as you need.

title: Off to Olympus
tagline: A rollicking reimagining of the legends of Greek mythology
synopsis: A bold, comedic reimagining of Greek mythology for the stage. Off to Olympus takes classic myths — gods, heroes, monsters, and all — and puts them in the hands of young performers with room to be big, physical, and funny. With 25 speaking roles plus a flexible chorus and a gender ratio that leans 3:1 toward girls, this is an ideal choice for middle and high school programs looking for challenging material with mythological scope and genuine comedy.
cast_notes: 25 speaking roles plus flexible chorus. Gender ratio leans approximately 3:1 toward girls.
licensing_notes: Unlimited performances within a 2-week calendar period
licensing_includes: Complete script (printable PDF — unlimited copies for your cast)
Vocal lead sheets for every song
Backing tracks for rehearsal and performance
Video recording and YouTube rights included
No script rentals. No returns. You keep everything.
notable_productions: The Actors Garden, Oak Park, Illinois

faq.q: Is this show appropriate for middle school?
faq.a: Yes. Off to Olympus is designed for 7th grade and up. The humor, themes, and vocal demands are calibrated for adolescent performers.

faq.q: Can I use this to complement a mythology curriculum unit?
faq.a: Absolutely. Many directors pair Off to Olympus with a classroom mythology unit. The show brings familiar myths to life in a way that deepens students' understanding of the source material.

faq.q: Why does the gender ratio lean toward girls?
faq.a: Many Greek mythology roles (goddesses, heroines, mythical figures) are female. The show was designed with this in mind, reflecting the reality that many youth programs have more female than male participants.
No. Complete backing tracks are included. The piano/vocal score is also provided if you have instrumentalists.

faq.q: This seems more challenging than your other shows. Is it?
faq.a: It is our most ambitious show — longer runtime, more songs, more complex staging possibilities. But the material is designed to challenge students at the right level, not overwhelm them. Most programs with experienced middle or high school casts handle it beautifully.

title: Pots and Pan
tagline: The next chapter in the story of the boy who refuses to grow up

title: Tut, Tut!
tagline: King Tut meets the Prince and the Pauper in this musical comedy
synopsis: A musical comedy that imagines what happens when the young pharaoh Tutankhamun encounters a common boy who looks just like him. Inspired by Mark Twain's The Prince and the Pauper but set in ancient Egypt, Tut, Tut! is a fast-paced 60-minute show perfect for younger performers.
source_material: The Prince and the Pauper / Ancient Egypt
licensing_notes: Licensed through Beat by Beat Press

title: Bounce!
tagline: A 30-minute musical about a kid with endless energy
synopsis: Bounce! follows Reggie, a 10-year-old whose boundless energy drives everyone around them a little crazy — until the community discovers that what looks like a problem might actually be a superpower. A short, energetic show ideal for younger casts and shorter attention spans.
licensing_notes: Licensed through Beat by Beat Press

title: The Show Must Go Online
tagline: The world's first virtual children's musical — performed by over 1,000 productions worldwide
synopsis: A virtual children's musical written in 19 days during the COVID-19 pandemic. The Show Must Go Online became the most-licensed musical of 2020, performed by over 1,000 productions in 40 countries. Written for remote performance, it captured a moment in time when theater kids everywhere needed a way to keep performing.
licensing_notes: Licensed through Beat by Beat Press. Most-licensed musical of 2020.
